what_input_feature: A 4-byte device-class / profile-class signature at offset 0x0c that the parser maps to XYZ-data PCS (winner seeds carry 'spac'=73 70 61 63 or 'prtr'=70 72 74 72), combined with the Lab output PCS selection, so InPCS==cmsSigXYZData and OutPCS==cmsSigLabData reach line 443.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds set offset 0x0c-0x0f to 'spac'/'prtr' (W bytes 70(p)/73(s) at 0x0c) and additionally flip the discriminator bytes at 0x2d (W=01) and 0x3d (W=50 'P'), which the loser never produces; these literal four-byte FOURCC class tags are exactly what I2SRandReplace can splice from a logged CMP operand.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds are stuck at offset 0x0c='mntr' (6d 6e 74 72) for all 20 occurrences and keep 0x2d=00, 0x3d=00, so the parser routes to a PCS path that never enters the XYZ->Lab arm at line 443.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where the parser compared the input class tag against the literal 'spac'/'prtr' FOURCC constants and substitutes that constant into the input bytes; value_profile has no I2SRandReplace and cannot place the exact multi-byte tag, so value_profile_cmplog beats value_profile on the I2S axis.

what_input_feature: Same XYZ-PCS-in / Lab-PCS-out structural requirement, but reached via incremental gradient progress on the multi-field colorspace comparison rather than a single substitution; the input must simultaneously satisfy the class tag and the colorspace fields (0x10-0x16) selecting Lab output.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 with hitcount 19 on the branch, getting CMP_MAP gradient credit for each colorspace/PCS sub-field that moves closer to the XYZ->Lab discriminant, layered on top of I2S substitution.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (no value profile) resolves only 2/10 with avg hitcount 1, so without the Hamming/prefix-distance feedback it rarely accumulates the multi-field colorspace state needed to enter the XYZ->Lab arm.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient preserves intermediate inputs that get closer on the colorspace/PCS comparisons, giving the fuzzer a smooth path to the XYZ->Lab discriminant; cmplog lacks this distance feedback, so value_profile_cmplog beats cmplog on the value_profile axis.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte device-class signature at offset 0x0c must equal a 4CC such as 'spac' or 'prtr' (and the colorspace fields at 0x10-0x16 must form a profile whose ColorSpaceIn is compatible with CurrentColorSpace) so the loop routes into the devicelink/abstract branch (line 576) and reaches the AddConversion call at line 592 with a non-NULL Lut.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry 'spac' (73 70 61 63) or 'prtr' (70 72 74 72) at offset 0x0c-0x0f and resolve the true branch with hitcount 19, taking the True side of line 592 (W T=12).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds all carry 'mntr' (6d 6e 74 72) at offset 0x0c-0x0f, never take the True side of line 592 (L T=0 F=38), and stay blocked with hitcount 0.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace over value_profile: the CMP interception logs the literal 4CC operands compared against the input (device-class / colorspace tag matches) and substitutes them at the matching input offsets, planting the exact 'spac'/'prtr' magic that value_profile's Hamming-bucket gradient alone never completes; the winning seed's lineage shows an I2S-floor dash row confirming an I2S find in the ancestor chain.

what_input_feature: The same device-class/colorspace byte configuration (device-class 4CC at 0x0c != 'mntr', compatible colorspace at 0x10-0x16) that routes into the devicelink branch and satisfies the ColorSpaceIsCompatible check at line 568, reaching AddConversion at line 592.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 with hitcount 19; its seeds reach the True side of the blocker (line 592 W T=12) after passing the colorspace-compatibility chain.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 2/10 and stays blocked 8/10 with avg hitcount 1, far below the winner, indicating its plain I2S substitution rarely assembles the full compatible-colorspace configuration the branch requires.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient over cmplog: each CMP-operand pair closer to matching adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus, giving a stepwise gradient toward the colorspace-compatibility comparisons that bare cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) cannot climb, so vpc compounds I2S substitution with gradient retention to reach the branch reliably.

what_input_feature: Profile-header bytes (device class FOURCC at offset 0x0c and color-space signatures at 0x10/0x14) that select a non-input, non-devicelink profile so the output-direction branch (line 602) runs _cmsReadOutputLUT and a subsequent ComputeConversion/AddConversion failure jumps to Error while Lut is non-NULL.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry varied device-class FOURCCs (link/prtr/spac) and color spaces (MCHC/GRAY/RGB/MCH1/Lab) at the header offsets, and per-role coverage shows the winner exclusively hitting the output-direction block (lines 562-566, 602-612) and the abstract/conversion path (583-592).
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds are uniformly 'mntr'+'RGB ' headers that take the input direction (line 597 lIsInput true), so Lut is freed at line 619 before any goto and the Error path never sees a non-NULL Lut.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes intercepted CMP operands (the FOURCC/colorspace literals compared inside cmsGetDeviceClass/ColorSpaceIsCompatible) into the input bytes, letting vpc construct the rare device-class/colorspace combination that drives the output-direction conversion failure; plain value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace.

what_input_feature: The same output-direction-failure profile, but here reached via gradient progress on the integer/string CMPs guarding the colorspace-compatibility and conversion checks rather than purely by substitution.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 80 on the branch; its CMP_MAP gradient feedback preserves intermediate inputs that get progressively closer to matching the colorspace signature CMPs (lines 568, 583), so the output-direction conversion path is reached and fails into Error.
why_loser_doesnt: plain cmplog resolves only 2/10 with avg hitcount 8; without CMP_MAP-bucket feedback it cannot retain near-miss inputs on the colorspace comparisons, so it rarely assembles the exact header combination that drives the output-direction failure.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback (Hamming-distance / matching-prefix buckets) preserves inputs that approach matching the colorspace/device-class comparisons as new corpus entries, supplying cmplog's substitution stage in vpc with stepping-stones; cmplog alone has only edge-bucket feedback and cannot ratchet toward the rare combination.

what_input_feature: A profile that parses far enough to read a tag back (device class 'link'/'abst', valid acsp magic, a non-Lut16 LUT tag) so that _cmsGetTagTrueType returns a tag type signature OTHER than cmsSigLut16Type. This requires several 4-byte signature fields (device class at 0x0c, colorspace fields at 0x10-0x17, and the tag-true-type discriminator) to hold specific structural values rather than the still-blocked 'mntr'/Lut16 layout.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 trials and reaches the true side (avg hitcount 14, blocked only 6.5h). Its winning seeds carry device-class bytes 'link' (offset 0x0c-0x0f) and varied colorspace signatures, and offset 0x2d flips to 01 vs the loser's 00 — distinct multi-byte signature substitutions consistent with I2SRandReplace splicing a logged tag-type/signature operand into the input at the offsets the parser compares.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile resolves 0/10, stays blocked all 24h with hitcount 0 on the branch. Its blocking seeds keep device class 'mntr' (offset 0x0c-0x0f) and offset 0x2d=00, so the tag read either fails or yields a Lut16 (mft2) true type, leaving OriginalType == cmsSigLut16Type and the true branch unreached.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace over value_profile. I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the input bytes matching one operand of an intercepted signature/type comparison, and substitutes the other operand, planting the exact 4-byte signature(s) the parser needs to make _cmsGetTagTrueType return a non-Lut16 type. value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient cannot place an exact multi-byte signature, so it never crosses.

what_input_feature: The same non-Lut16-true-type profile structure, but reached via incremental Hamming/prefix-distance progress on the chain of signature/equality comparisons (device class, colorspace, tag type) so that intermediate near-matches are retained as corpus and refined toward the exact non-Lut16 layout.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 vs cmplog's 2/10 (prob_div 0.70, hit_div 8); its CMP_MAP feedback preserves seeds whose 4-byte signature fields (0x0c-0x12) are progressively closer to a valid non-Lut16 tag layout, building the corpus that the I2S stage then completes.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 2/10 and stays blocked ~20h with branch hitcount 6 — it has I2S but lacks the CMP_MAP gradient, so near-miss signature seeds that don't change an edge bucket are discarded and the search loses the partial-progress scaffold value_profile_cmplog keeps.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient over cmplog. Each signature/type CMP writes a (PC, distance-bucket) entry; an input whose operand pair is closer to matching than any seen adds a new bucket and is preserved as corpus. This retains the intermediate near-match profiles that cmplog throws away, giving the combined fuzzer a refinable foothold on the multi-field signature gate.

what_input_feature: Bytes that survive ICC header signature/colour-space checks and place a non-zero offset payload so the matrix stage's Data->Offset pointer is non-NULL; W seeds carry the 'lams'/'link' profile class and non-zero bytes at offsets 0x2d (01) and 0x38 (16) where the loser holds zero.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog seeds reach the true side: avg hitcount on branch winner=29, loser=0, and the byte diff shows winner-only 01 at 0x2d and 16 at 0x38 (loser is 00 at both), the dataflow that yields a populated Offset.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile never resolves (resolved=0/10, blocked=10); its seeds hold 00 at 0x2d and 0x38 and the 'lcms'/'mntr' header bytes, so the matrix stage is built without an Offset and the true branch is never taken.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the matching ICC magic/signature operands into the input at the right offsets, letting value_profile_cmplog clear the header CMPs that gate construction of an offset-bearing matrix stage; value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot place those operands.

what_input_feature: Same offset-bearing matrix-stage requirement, but reached by gradually closing the distance on the ICC header/signature comparisons rather than by exact substitution.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 vs cmplog 2/10, with branch hitcount winner=29 vs loser=13; the CMP_MAP gradient buckets let it ratchet toward the signature/colour-space bytes (0x05, 0x0c-0x0f) that select an offset-bearing stage.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 8/10 and stays at hitcount 13 with avg blocked duration 20.20h; without CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback it has no gradient to preserve near-miss inputs and rarely assembles the offset payload.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per CMP and preserves any input that gets a comparand pair closer to matching; this gradient feedback, present only in value_profile_cmplog within this pair, drives the winner past the header gates that cmplog (edge-bucket only) cannot incrementally solve.

what_input_feature: A valid 4-byte ICC profile-class FOURCC at offset 0x0c (e.g. 'link','prtr','spac','lcms') plus a recognized device/colorspace tag, so DefaultICCintents builds a non-empty Lut whose stages cmsPipelineCat then dups into l1 via cmsPipelineInsertStage.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry varied non-default profile-class signatures at 0x0c-0x0f ('lcms','link','prtr','spac','MCH1'), and the vpc winner's lineage contains an I2S-floor dash row that splices logged CMP operands into these FOURCC offsets, producing inputs that traverse the stage-dup loop and take the blocked true side.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; its loser-blocking seeds are uniformly 'mntr' at 0x0c-0x0f (6d 6e 74 72) and never substitute the alternate profile-class operands needed, so the pipeline stays in a shape that does not reach the InsertStage true branch (0/10 resolved).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the logged comparand for the profile-class FOURCC check at the input offset, which value_profile (no I2S stage) cannot perform.

what_input_feature: The same multi-profile FOURCC/colorspace fields, but reached via incremental Hamming-distance progress on the profile-class and colorspace CMP operands rather than a single exact substitution.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP gradient on top of I2S, so partial matches on the 0x0c-0x16 tag bytes earn new CMP_MAP buckets and are retained as corpus, letting it resolve 9/10 with 50 hits versus cmplog's 5 hits.
why_loser_doesnt: plain cmplog has no CMP_MAP feedback; without per-bucket distance credit it cannot accumulate the partial FOURCC/colorspace progress and resolves only 2/10 trials (loser blocked=8).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets) rewards inputs that bring the profile-class/colorspace comparands closer to matching, a feedback signal cmplog lacks.

what_input_feature: Exact 4-byte FOURCC profile-class and colorspace tokens at offsets 0x0c-0x16 (e.g. 'prtr', 'Lab ') that must equal hard-coded signature constants the parser compares against, steering execution to a cmsPipelineAlloc invocation whose InputChannels argument reaches cmsMAXCHANNELS so line 1373's true side is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: The winning vpc seed carries 'prtr' at 0x0c-0x0f and 'Lab Lab ' at 0x10-0x17, and is the only seed that takes the true side (L1373 T=1 in the W role); its lineage contains an I2S-floor dash row, so I2SRandReplace spliced a logged CMP operand into these signature offsets.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator; its blocking seeds keep the generic 'mntr'/'RGB '/'XYZ ' signatures at those offsets and never flip line 1373 to true (L1373 T=0 F=492 in the L role).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact signature constant at the matching input offset, instantly satisfying the multi-byte FOURCC equality that value_profile (which lacks the mutator) cannot place by chance.

what_input_feature: The same FOURCC signature bytes at offsets 0x0c-0x16, but approached as a multi-byte comparison whose matching-prefix length must grow incrementally before the exact match is reached.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner adds value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient on top of cmplog; the byte-diff shows the winner converged to a single concentrated value ('prtr','Lab ') at these offsets while losers retain a spread, consistent with a prefix-distance gradient retaining intermediate near-matches as corpus.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has the edge+CMP buffer but no CMP_MAP bucket feedback, so it cannot retain partial-prefix progress on the signature comparisons; it stays blocked 10/10 with avg duration 24.00h versus the winner's 7.40h.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a matching-prefix-length bucket per string/memory CMP, preserving inputs that move closer to the target FOURCC; this gradient is the technique cmplog lacks, so the vpc>cmplog divergence is attributable to the CMP_MAP gradient axis.

what_input_feature: Input bytes that drive xmlRegexpCompile far enough that an existing automaton state is passed as the epsilon-transition target, so the false side of `if (to == NULL)` is taken; reaching this requires solving the chain of regexp/schema-facet structural comparisons (atom min/max/quant fields at d=2) that gate the deeper automaton-construction path.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient feedback on top of cmplog, so inputs that bring the regexp-parser CMP operands (atom->min/max/quant comparisons) closer to matching are preserved as corpus and incrementally driven to the construction path; the winner resolves the branch in 8/10 trials and hits the false side (avg hitcount=3) where cmplog does not (hitcount=0).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S but no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so it lacks the Hamming/prefix distance signal that preserves near-matching parser states; it stays on the true (allocation) side, resolving only 1/10 with avg hitcount 0 on the branch.
mechanism_attribution: The value_profile delta supplies the CMP_MAP gradient: distance-bucket feedback on the regexp-parser integer comparisons gives a smooth gradient toward the configuration that yields a non-null target state, which plain cmplog edge feedback cannot reward incrementally.

what_input_feature: Input bytes that satisfy the exact-literal regexp/schema tokens needed to construct the automaton with a pre-existing target state, requiring substitution of logged comparand values into the input (e.g. the literal DOCTYPE/version XML scaffolding and facet pattern bytes) so the parser proceeds to the deeper epsilon-transition path.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog has I2SRandReplace; its winning seed's ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows, evidence that input-to-state substitution placed required comparand bytes that drove the parser to the non-null-target path (winner resolves 8/10, hitcount=3).
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile has CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace, so it cannot directly splice logged comparand operands into the input to satisfy the exact-literal parser checks; it resolves only 1/10 with avg hitcount 1.
mechanism_attribution: The I2S delta supplies I2SRandReplace: it reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the matching operand at the offsets where the other operand appears, jumping the exact-literal parser comparisons that value_profile's gradient alone does not clear.

what_input_feature: Input must be a syntactically valid XML document with a DOCTYPE declaration and a DTD element content model containing an optional ('?') quantifier (XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_OPT), so xmlAutomataNewEpsilon is called with a non-NULL 'to' state and the false branch of 'if (to == NULL)' at line 6340 is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner (value_profile_cmplog) resolves 8/10 and hits the branch with avg hitcount 3; its per-role coverage takes the OPT case (line 671 case XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_OPT marked [W]) and reaches line 6342 return(to), the false-branch sink marked [W]. CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback rewards partial-match progress through the many string comparisons in the DTD/validation path (xmlIsDocNameChar, xmlGetDtdElementDesc), building the structured prefix needed to reach the OPT-quantified content model.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser (cmplog) blocks 9/10 with avg hitcount 0 on the branch; its inputs drive the XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_MULT case (line 682 marked [L]) which calls xmlAutomataNewEpsilon with NULL 'to', taking the true branch (line 6341 return(am->state) marked [L]). Lacking CMP_MAP gradient feedback, cmplog gets no incremental reward for partial matches and does not converge on the OPT content-model bytes.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming-distance / matching-prefix buckets) is the only technique value_profile_cmplog has that cmplog lacks; it preserves inputs that bring DTD-parsing comparisons closer to matching, guiding the fuzzer toward the optional-quantifier content model that supplies a non-NULL automaton target state.

what_input_feature: Same structural feature: a valid DOCTYPE/DTD whose element content model uses the optional ('?') quantifier so xmlAutomataNewEpsilon receives a non-NULL 'to' and takes the false branch at line 6340.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner (value_profile_cmplog) resolves 8/10 with avg hitcount 3 and reaches line 6342 return(to) [W] via the OPT case [W]. Its winning seed 4bed379e31cead31 carries the literal XML scaffolding bytes (<?xml version="1.0"?> and <!DOCTYPE) and its ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows, indicating I2SRandReplace spliced logged comparison operands (DTD keyword/element literals) into the input to satisfy the string comparisons gating the content-model path.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser (value_profile) blocks 9/10 with avg hitcount 1; lacking I2SRandReplace it cannot directly substitute logged CMP operands and so fails to reliably assemble the exact DTD literals needed to drive the OPT content model that yields a non-NULL automaton target.
mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace (present in value_profile_cmplog, absent in value_profile) reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the matching operand at the input offset, placing the exact DTD/element literals required to reach the optional-quantifier branch; this is the single technique delta between the pair.

what_input_feature: Bytes that drive the libxml2 parser to allocate an XML_CDATA_SECTION_NODE, i.e. a recognizable multi-byte '<![CDATA[' literal embedded in a well-formed document so the saver's switch dispatches to case XML_CDATA_SECTION_NODE at line 988.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 and actually executes the CDATA arm (entry-line hits>=1; the W column shows L988 onward marked [W]); the CMP_MAP gradient feedback preserves intermediate inputs that get progressively closer to matching the parser's multi-byte token comparisons, building the '<![CDATA[' / '<?xml' / '<!DOCTYPE' skeleton seen in the winning seed.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 1/10 and is blocked 9/10; lacking the CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback it gets no reward for partial matches of the multi-byte CDATA token, so it never reliably assembles the literal and the saver dispatches to other case arms (L988 stays False=194 on the L side).
mechanism_attribution: The decisive technique is the value_profile CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient unique to value_profile_cmplog over cmplog; it rewards inputs whose CMP operands edge toward matching the parser's CDATA/element token comparisons, letting the corpus climb toward a valid '<![CDATA[' construct that cmplog (no CMP_MAP) cannot ratchet toward.

what_input_feature: The same requirement: bytes parsing into a tree with an XML_CDATA_SECTION_NODE, which depends on placing the exact multi-byte literal '<![CDATA[' (and surrounding well-formed XML) into the input.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10; its I2SRandReplace mutator reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and splices the exact comparand the parser tests for (the CDATA/element token bytes) directly into the input at matching offsets, so the precise '<![CDATA[' literal appears verbatim. The winning seed's ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows, confirming an I2S find.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile resolves only 2/10 and is blocked 8/10; it has no I2SRandReplace mutator (havoc+token only), so it must stumble onto the exact multi-byte CDATA literal by chance and rarely does, leaving the saver to dispatch to other case arms.
mechanism_attribution: The decisive technique is cmplog's I2SRandReplace input-to-state substitution present in value_profile_cmplog but absent in value_profile; it copies the logged CDATA/element comparand into the input at matching offsets, directly synthesizing the literal token the parser needs to build a CDATA node.

what_input_feature: Input must produce an element node that already has a first namespace (node->nsDef != NULL) and then declare a SECOND, distinctly-prefixed namespace on the same element, requiring well-formed multi-byte tokens like "<?xml version=\"1.0\"?>", "<!DOCTYPE", and a parseable xmlns attribute chain so SAX2 calls xmlNewNs twice on the same node.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile_cmplog winner seeds carry an intact, in-order XML prologue ("<?xml version=\"1.0\"?>", "<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM") at offsets 0x08+, which the I2SRandReplace mutator assembled by substituting CMP operands; the prologue parses far enough to drive a second xmlNewNs call and take the false (non-NULL nsDef) side, hitting it 6 times on average.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser seeds are shorter and structurally mangled (e.g. 97/156-byte seeds with byte runs like 0x13 repeats and corrupted prologue ordering), so the parser at most reaches the first namespace declaration; the loser only resolves the branch in 2/10 trials with avg hitcount 1.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and splices logged comparison operands (XML keyword/token bytes) into the input at matching offsets, repairing the multi-byte structural tokens that gate reaching a second xmlNewNs call; value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot reconstruct those exact token sequences.

what_input_feature: Same structural target (an element whose node->nsDef is already non-NULL when a second xmlns is declared), but here the winning edge is value-profile gradient feedback that preserves intermediate inputs whose CMP operands are progressively closer to matching the XML structural literals, letting the corpus climb toward a parseable two-namespace document.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 trials versus cmplog's 0/10; the CMP_MAP bucket feedback retains near-miss inputs (Hamming/prefix-distance closer to the xmlns and prologue token comparisons), so the corpus accumulates the structural progress needed to reach and append the second namespace at the blocker false side (avg hitcount winner=6, loser=0).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has the same I2SRandReplace mutator but only the edge-bucket feedback signal; it never retains the gradient intermediates, so across all 10 trials it stays blocked (resolved=0/10, blocked=10) and the branch hitcount is 0.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: each integer/string CMP writes a distance-bucket into CMP_MAP, and an input producing an operand pair closer to matching than any seen before is preserved as new coverage; this gradient is the only technique differing between vpc and cmplog and is what lets the corpus accumulate toward the two-namespace document.

what_input_feature: A self-closing empty element whose start tag declares at least one namespace (e.g. a prefixed/xmlns attribute) so that ctxt->nsNr is incremented before the `/>` is consumed, making nsNr != ctxt->nsNr true at line 10185.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner assembles a well-formed XML prolog plus angle-bracketed markup (`?>.<!`, `version="1.0"?>`, `<!DOCTY`) so the parser advances into xmlParseStartTag2/xmlParseElementStart and reaches the empty-element close where the namespace count diverges; vpc's I2SRandReplace splices logged comparands (element/attribute name and `xmlns`-style literal bytes) into the input so the upstream name/CMP gates pass.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser (no I2S) blocks 10/10 and never takes the true side; lacking I2SRandReplace it cannot substitute the exact logged literal bytes that the upstream name/attribute comparisons require, so it rarely builds an empty element that actually declares a namespace.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching input offsets, planting the exact literal bytes needed by upstream name/markup comparisons so the parser reaches the namespaced empty-element close; this technique is present in vpc but absent in the value_profile loser.

what_input_feature: The same namespaced empty element, but reached via incremental progress on the upstream byte-level comparisons (matching prefixes of markup literals such as <?xml, DOCTYPE, attribute names and quote/`/>` delimiters).
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner retains intermediate inputs that get progressively closer to the required markup literals; the CMP_MAP gradient preserves a seed whenever a CMP-operand pair is closer to matching than any seen before, letting vpc climb toward the multi-character markup tokens (`<!DOCTY`, `version=`) that gate entry to the empty-element branch.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser (no value_profile) blocks 9/10; it has I2S substitution but no Hamming/prefix-distance feedback, so partially-matching markup inputs earn no new coverage bucket and are discarded, and it stalls before assembling the full namespaced empty tag.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a coarse operand-distance bucket per CMP and rewards inputs that get closer to matching, preserving partial-match intermediates as corpus; this gradient is present in vpc but absent in the cmplog loser, so only vpc keeps climbing the multi-byte markup comparisons.
